mappings.rationaleAI: Theatre integrating dance with literature, music, and painting addresses the integration of dance with other arts (A.5).
Ethnomusicology covering connections between music and dance addresses primitive, folk, and popular dance (D).
Aesthetics examining art, beauty, and aesthetic experience addresses theoretical and critical writing about dance (A.4).
Ritual encompassing rites of passage and ceremonies connects to the origins and functions of dance (A.1) and primitive and folk dance (D).
Anthropology studying cultural systems through ethnography provides context for primitive, folk, and popular dance (D).
Global folk music traditions including Uzbek dance tunes address primitive, folk, and popular dance (D).
Modern theatrical drama covering the relationship between performance and innovation addresses the integration of dance with other arts in the theatre (A.5).
Music covering performance practice and participatory music-making addresses the integration of dance with other arts (A.5).
Cinema as art form addresses the integration of dance with motion pictures (A.5).
Creativity covering creative expression across cultures provides foundational context for the art of dance (A).

Covers non-Western and folk musical traditions globally, which directly intersects with primitive, folk, and popular dance traditions, as music and dance are deeply intertwined in folk and indigenous performance contexts.
